text.title: go-http-handlers Failure Modes
text.summary: Five antipatterns each with symptom / root-cause / fix.

antipattern.description: gin.Default() instead of gin.New() with explicit middleware.
antipattern.symptom: Log format does not match team conventions; trace IDs missing from logs; double Recovery if you also add your own.
antipattern.root-cause: gin.Default bundles a generic Logger + Recovery; teams add their own and end up with two.
antipattern.fix: Replace gin.Default with gin.New + explicit r.Use(mw.Recovery, mw.RequestID, mw.Logger, ...).

antipattern.symptom: Slow request continues after client disconnect; resource usage doesn't drop on cancellation; trace IDs lost.
antipattern.root-cause: Background detaches from request ctx; cancellation does not propagate.
antipattern.fix: Use c.Request.Context() (Gin) or echo.Request().Context() (Echo). For genuine background work, derive `context.WithoutCancel(c.Request.Context())` and add a timeout.

antipattern.description: Spawning a goroutine inside a handler that captures c (gin.Context) and uses c.Request.Context() later.
antipattern.symptom: Sporadic `context canceled` errors in background work; race-detector flags data races on gin.Context.
antipattern.root-cause: gin.Context is request-scoped and not thread-safe; the ctx it carries dies when handler returns.
antipattern.fix: Extract needed values before `go func`; derive a detached ctx with explicit timeout; pass values + ctx by argument.

antipattern.description: Business logic embedded in handler body (multi-branch validation, DB calls, transformation).
antipattern.symptom: Handler files grow past 100 lines; unit tests require spinning up the HTTP stack to exercise rules.
antipattern.root-cause: No enforced thinness rule; agents add logic where the test passes the input.
antipattern.fix: Extract logic into service methods. Handler becomes bind → validate-binding → service-call → translate-to-http; keep under 40 lines.

antipattern.description: http.Server constructed without ReadHeaderTimeout/ReadTimeout/WriteTimeout/IdleTimeout (defaults to zero == unbounded).
antipattern.symptom: Slow-client connections (Slowloris) hold file descriptors; fd exhaustion under load.
antipattern.root-cause: Defaults are zero; one-line `r.Run(":8080")` does not configure them.
antipattern.fix: Use templates/server.go shape: set all four timeouts and call srv.ListenAndServe.
